name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
description: EU regulation on the processing and free movement of personal data. Principles are encoded at article level; detailed sub-requirements are intentionally summarised.

principles.title: Principles relating to processing of personal data
principles.summary: Personal data must be processed lawfully, fairly and transparently, collected for specified purposes, minimised, accurate, kept no longer than necessary, and processed securely, with the controller accountable.
queries: data minimisation and accuracy of personal data
how long personal data is retained
principles.expects: The document states the purposes of processing, limits collection to what is needed, and sets retention limits.

principles.title: Lawfulness of processing
principles.summary: Processing requires a lawful basis such as consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, or legitimate interests.
queries: legal basis for processing personal data
principles.expects: The document identifies the lawful basis relied on for each processing purpose.

principles.title: Conditions for consent
principles.summary: Where consent is the basis, it must be demonstrable, freely given, specific, informed, unambiguous, and as easy to withdraw as to give.
queries: how consent is obtained and recorded
principles.expects: The document explains how consent is collected, recorded, and withdrawn.

principles.title: Transparency and information to data subjects
principles.summary: Individuals must receive concise, transparent, intelligible privacy information, including identity of the controller, purposes, recipients, retention, and their rights.
queries: privacy notice information provided to individuals
who controls personal data and how to contact them
principles.expects: The document provides or references a privacy notice covering controller identity, purposes, recipients, retention and rights.

principles.title: Data subject rights
principles.summary: Individuals have rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction, data portability, and objection, including in relation to automated decision-making.
principles.expects: The document describes the rights individuals can exercise and how requests are handled.

principles.title: Processors
principles.summary: Processing by a third party on the controller's behalf must be governed by a contract imposing data-protection obligations on the processor.
queries: third party service providers processing data
principles.expects: The document addresses vendor/processor arrangements and contractual data-protection obligations.

principles.title: Records of processing activities
principles.summary: Controllers and processors must maintain records of processing activities, including purposes, categories, recipients, transfers, and retention.
principles.expects: The document commits to maintaining records or a register of processing activities.

principles.title: Security of processing
principles.summary: Appropriate technical and organisational measures must protect personal data, considering pseudonymisation, encryption, confidentiality, integrity, availability, and testing.
principles.expects: The document describes concrete security measures protecting personal data.

principles.title: Personal data breach notification
principles.summary: Breaches must be notified to the supervisory authority without undue delay (where feasible within 72 hours) and to affected individuals when high risk.
queries: notifying regulator and individuals after a breach
principles.expects: The document sets out a breach response and notification procedure with timeframes.

principles.title: Data protection impact assessment
principles.summary: A DPIA is required before processing likely to result in high risk to individuals, such as large-scale profiling or monitoring.
queries: assessing privacy risk before new processing
principles.expects: The document requires impact assessments for high-risk processing.

principles.title: Data protection officer
principles.summary: A DPO must be designated in certain cases and involved in data-protection matters, with independence and adequate resources.
principles.expects: The document designates a DPO or privacy officer and describes their role.

principles.title: International transfers
principles.summary: Transfers outside the EU/EEA require an adequacy decision or appropriate safeguards such as standard contractual clauses or binding corporate rules.
queries: international data transfer safeguards standard contractual clauses
principles.expects: The document identifies overseas transfers and the safeguards relied on.
